hero.tagline: I build robust APIs and scalable applications with Symfony and Django. I care about clean code, best practices and systems that don't fail in production.

about.label: 01. About
about.title: Who I am
paragraphs: I'm Gonzalo Jiménez, a Backend Developer at Codearts Solutions. I work mainly with Symfony, Angular and React Native on projects for the leisure and hospitality sectors. Over time I've taken on a technical lead role in small teams, which has led me to combine hands-on development with coordination and direct client contact.

I came to software development looking for a discipline I could grow in professionally — one that combined two things I was drawn to: technology and logical problem-solving. I chose a vocational higher degree because I wanted practical training and to enter the workforce quickly so I could learn on the job. I haven't ruled out a full engineering degree down the line, but for now I'm making the most of this stage of real-world learning.

What hooks me most about programming is the moment a client presents a problem or a need and you have to figure out the best way to solve it with the resources available. I'm especially drawn to complex projects where you don't just develop — you also have to think about how to fit the solution within the constraints and requirements you've been given.

My approach is fairly pragmatic: I believe in best practices and writing tests, but I prioritise working software. I'd rather have something imperfect that solves the problem and can be refined later than something theoretically flawless that delivers no real value to the client.

As for what's next: I don't have a rigid plan. My goal is to build the knowledge and skills that give me the freedom to choose what I want to do at any given moment — while continuing to learn and work on projects that push me out of my comfort zone.

bullets: Backend development and technical lead on projects for the leisure and hospitality sectors.
Direct client coordination and technical decision-making within small teams.
Key projects: Making Of, Museo Cartuja, DPTelemetry.
Internship within the team assigned to Repsol for the development of the internal admin portal of WiBLE, a car-sharing service in Madrid (a joint venture between Repsol and Kia).
Development of new features and improvement of existing ones using Python, Django and PostgreSQL, with occasional frontend tasks in JavaScript.
Docker-based development environment within a team following Scrum and Kanban methodologies.

entries.title: WiBLE — Admin Portal
entries.description: Internal administration portal for WiBLE, a car-sharing service in Madrid (joint venture between Repsol and Kia). Developed during an internship at Ayesa within the team assigned to Repsol.
highlights: Development of new features and improvement of existing ones in the admin tool using Python and Django.
PostgreSQL integration and occasional frontend tasks in JavaScript.
Docker environment with Scrum and Kanban methodologies in a real corporate client team.

entries.title: DPTelemetry App
entries.description: Real-time telemetry mobile app for motorsport competitions. Allows drivers and teams to monitor race sessions, visualise GPS routes on a map and follow the live leaderboard during events.
highlights: Live map with up to 5 device positions updated at 20 Hz via WebSocket.
Session history with paginated telemetry data, lap times and advanced filters.
Full auth flow with JWT, automatic token refresh and secure storage via expo-secure-store.

entries.title: MakkingOf
entries.description: Full redesign of the MakkingOf web platform, a company in the leisure and hospitality sector. Includes a public landing page, an event request and management system with differentiated roles (user, company, admin) and automated email notifications.
highlights: REST API documented with OpenAPI/Swagger and validated in a GitLab CI pipeline on every push.
Soft delete with automatic purge after 90 days via Symfony Scheduler and Messenger.
VPS deployment with Docker Compose, Nginx and automatic SSL certificate via Certbot.

entries.title: Museo Cartuja
entries.description: Redesign of the Museo Cartuja de Murcia web platform. Angular application with PWA capabilities for offline use, Google social login integration and a Stripe payment gateway for ticket sales.
highlights: PWA with Service Worker and asset caching: the app works offline and loads from cache on repeat visits.
Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 social login integrated in the Symfony backend.
Stripe payment gateway for ticket and booking management.

entries.title: Higher National Diploma in Web Application Development
entries.description: Vocational Higher Degree (equivalent to HND). Training in full stack web development, databases, application deployment and software development methodologies.

contact.label: 06. Contact
contact.title: Let's talk
contact.description: If you have an interesting opportunity, a question or just want to connect, drop me a message. I reply within 24 hours.
contact.message_placeholder: Tell me how I can help...
contact.success: Message sent. I'll get back to you soon.
